title: Oracle Corp. stock rises Monday, outperforms market
summary: Oracle Corp. (ORCL) stock increased by 2.02% to $89.28 on Monday, outperforming the broader market downturn where the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average fell. This marks the stock's second consecutive day of gains, although it remains $17.06 short of its 52-week high. Oracle's performance was mixed compared to competitors like Microsoft, Adobe, and Salesforce.com, and trading volume exceeded its 50-day average.

title: ServiceNow crowned Glassdoor’s best place to work in UK
summary: Software company ServiceNow has been named the UK's best place to work in 2022 by Glassdoor, based on positive anonymous employee feedback regarding its culture, opportunities, and training. Technology companies dominated the list, with seven in the top ten, reflecting a shift in employee expectations post-pandemic. Glassdoor's 2022 methodology also included employers' diversity and inclusion ratings for the first time.

title: Content Marketing Market to grow by USD 417.85 billion from 2020 to 2025 | Evolving Opportunities with Adobe Inc. & Contently, Inc.| 17000+ Technavio Reports
summary: The content marketing market is projected to grow by USD 417.85 billion from 2020 to 2025, driven largely by the increasing number of social media users. The integration of AI with social media management software is a key trend supporting market growth, although digital advertisement fraud poses a significant challenge. Technavio's report highlights opportunities across various end-users and geographies, with key players including Adobe Inc. and Contently, Inc.

title: Employee surveillance is exploding with remote work—and could be the new norm
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic significantly accelerated the adoption of employee surveillance software, with 60% of large companies using these technologies by the end of 2021, compared to 30% pre-pandemic. This expansion of monitoring into employees' homes raises significant concerns about privacy, trust, and the blurring of work-life boundaries. Experts warn of "function creep," where temporary surveillance measures become permanent, and highlight the urgent need for new legal protections for workers' privacy, as existing laws are largely weak and inadequate for the evolving landscape of remote work.

title: LIVE MARKETS Digitization to drive software demand
summary: This article from Reuters examines various market dynamics, including the digitization driving software demand, the strong performance of real estate and challenges faced by the airline industry. It also includes analyst insights on potential market corrections and the impact of mid-term elections on market volatility, alongside housing market data for the U.S.

title: How UX Design Fuels Space Exploration at NASA & SpaceX
summary: This article explores how UX design is crucial for space exploration at NASA and SpaceX, focusing on human-centered design principles. Designers like Krys Blackwood, Tiffany Truong, and Amy Ross emphasize the importance of user research, iterative prototyping, and designing for extreme environments to create efficient tools and systems for astronauts, scientists, and engineers. Their work ranges from designing intuitive software interfaces for spacecraft and rovers to developing flexible spacesuits, all aimed at improving mission success and user productivity.

title: Instant Ump: HP Inc's subscription ink services hiking prices from next month
summary: HP is increasing the UK prices for its Instant Ink subscription service for specific tiers, with some customers facing hikes of over 50%. The price changes, effective February 26, 2022, primarily impact the "Occasional" and "Moderate" plans. While HP attributes the adjustments to market conditions, some customers are displeased and the company has not publicly detailed its pricing strategy.

title: Adobe Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
summary: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) stock declined by 1.55% on Thursday, closing at $493.05, amid a general downturn in the stock market where the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also fell. This marks its third consecutive day of losses, leaving the stock $206.49 below its 52-week high. While Adobe underperformed some rivals like Microsoft Corp. and Salesforce.com Inc., it still fared better than SAP SE ADR.

title: 20 years of transparency in PDF
summary: This article commemorates the 20th anniversary of partial transparency being introduced into the Adobe PDF 1.4 specification in 2001, highlighting it as a significant technical advancement for page description languages. It explains how native transparency transformed graphic design by enabling effects like drop shadows and feathering, providing greater creativity and device independence compared to previous opaque models. The article also touches on the technical challenges and eventual widespread adoption that made transparency a core feature of the portable document experience.

title: Adobe XD Review
summary: Adobe XD is a powerful and intuitive design software for interactive interface design and prototyping, recognized as an Editors' Choice. It excels due to its seamless integration with other Creative Cloud apps and robust responsive design tools. While Sketch is Mac-only, Adobe XD supports both macOS and Windows, making it a versatile option for UI/UX designers, despite having fewer plug-ins than its competitor.

title: Metaverse stocks Nvidia and Roblox fall as Facebook parent Meta's Web3 business reports massive loss
summary: Shares of metaverse-related companies like Roblox, Nvidia, and Matterport fell after Meta Platforms reported a significant operating loss of $10 billion in its Web3 division, Reality Labs, for 2021. The news led to a market value wipeout of over $200 billion for Meta and a sell-off across various tech stocks connected to the metaverse concept. This downturn highlights concerns following Meta's aggressive push into the metaverse space.

title: Adobe's Video Editor Can Now Intelligently Remix and Shorten Music to Perfectly Fit the Length of a Clip
summary: Adobe Premiere Pro has introduced a new AI-powered Remix Tool, utilizing Adobe Sensei, which can intelligently edit and shorten music tracks to precisely match the length of a video clip. This tool analyzes existing music and re-assembles it, preserving the start and finish while adjusting the overall timing, thus offering a efficient solution for video editors. While it doesn't base edits on video content, it provides sliders for editors to fine-tune the remixing process to achieve the desired tone or action match.

title: Coca-Cola creates 3D designs in days with Substance 3D
summary: Coca-Cola is leveraging Adobe Substance 3D Stager to dramatically accelerate its design process, creating dozens of 3D designs in days instead of weeks, saving over $200,000 in photography costs, and completing 100% of creative in-house. This digital workflow, exemplified by the Topo Chico Hard Seltzer launch, enables faster iterations, more experimentation, and photorealistic renders, setting a new standard for their design and marketing efforts. The company aims to preserve its creative legacy while innovating for the future, with 3D design being crucial for their physical products in a digital world.

title: If you use these Adobe apps, you may be under ‘high’ risk
summary: The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has issued a high-severity warning for users of Adobe After Effects and Adobe Creative Cloud. The warning highlights vulnerabilities in these applications, specifically an out-of-bounds write issue in After Effects and an uncontrolled search path element issue in Creative Cloud, which could allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code. Users are advised to update their Adobe apps to the latest versions to mitigate these risks.

title: Aprimo Named a Leader in DAM for Customer Experience by Independent Research Firm
summary: Aprimo has been recognized as a 'Leader' in "The Forrester Wave™: Digital Asset Management (DAM) for Customer Experience, Q1 2022" report, achieving top rankings in Current Offering and Strategy categories. The report highlighted Aprimo's strong AI, content atomization, and workflow capabilities, citing its product vision to support the full content performance lifecycle. Aprimo earned the highest possible score in 21 out of 27 criteria, demonstrating its commitment to innovation and providing a best-in-class solution for content operations.

title: Insight Names Alexander L. Baum as Board Director
summary: Insight Enterprises has appointed Alexander L. Baum, a partner at ValueAct Capital and a significant investor in the company, as an independent director to its board. This addition aims to leverage Baum's extensive experience in IT industry strategy and investment expertise to further Insight's transformation into a leading solutions integrator. ValueAct Capital expresses excitement about deepening its relationship with Insight, believing its transformation in response to the changing IT landscape is underappreciated.

title: What is Adobe?
summary: Adobe Systems, officially known as Adobe, is an American software company renowned for its multimedia and creativity software products like Photoshop, Acrobat Reader, and Creative Cloud. Founded in 1982 by John Warnock and Charles Geschke, the company first introduced digital fonts and later consumer software like Adobe Illustrator and its flagship product, Photoshop. The article also touches on the Adobe Creative Cloud suite and past data breaches that exposed user information.

title: Nokia Shanghai Bell Philippines, Inc. speeds connections with customers using Adobe Acrobat Sign
summary: Nokia Shanghai Bell Philippines, Inc. (NSBPH) implemented Adobe Acrobat Sign to streamline its customer documentation and signature processes, particularly for acceptance certificates involving multiple stakeholders across the Philippines. The solution enabled NSBPH to move from a manual, paper-based system to an efficient electronic workflow, ensuring timely revenue collection and project approvals. This digital transformation proved especially crucial during the pandemic, allowing the company to maintain operations and secure necessary sign-offs even with teams and customers working remotely.

title: Adobe named a Leader for fifth consecutive year in 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Digital Experience Platforms
summary: Adobe has been recognized as a Leader for the fifth consecutive year in the 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Digital Experience Platforms, positioned highest and furthest to the right for both Ability to Execute and Completeness of Vision. The company highlights its market-leading technology in integrated insights, AI/ML (Adobe Sensei), content management, scale and breadth (handling 21.5 trillion segment evaluations daily), real-time personalization, and the foundational Adobe Experience Platform. Adobe emphasizes its commitment to helping businesses deliver personalized customer experiences at scale, highlighting trust, data privacy, and efficient workflow management with Adobe Workfront as critical components for success.

title: What’s Changed: 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Experience Platforms
summary: This article summarizes the key takeaways from Gartner's 2022 Magic Quadrant for Digital Experience Platforms (DXP), detailing the evolution of DXPs and their core capabilities. It provides an overview of the criteria for vendors to qualify for the report and categorizes prominent DXP providers into Leaders, Challengers, Niche Players, and Visionaries, highlighting their strengths and specialties.

title: Ryan Reynolds, Kristen Bell, John Donahoe, and more join the Adobe Summit lineup
summary: The article announces the star-studded lineup for Adobe Summit 2022, featuring influential business leaders, innovators, and changemakers. Speakers include Ryan Reynolds, Kristen Bell, John Donahoe (Nike), Rosalind Brewer (Walgreens Boots Alliance), Lorenzo Bertelli (Prada Group), Gail McGovern (American Red Cross), and Adobe's CEO Shantanu Narayen and Anil Chakravarthy. The event will focus on customer experiences in the digital economy and showcase new innovations.

title: Adobe Named One of the World’s Most Innovative Companies in AI by Fast Company
summary: Adobe has been recognized by Fast Company as one of the "World's Most Innovative Companies" for 2022 in the AI category, ranking No. 4, due to its work with Adobe Sensei. Adobe Sensei is the AI/ML technology that powers hundreds of capabilities across their products, enhancing creativity and accelerating business processes. The company emphasizes an ethical approach to AI development, guided by principles of accountability, responsibility, and transparency.

title: Adobe Acrobat Sign and Adobe Experience Cloud modernize customer and employee experiences
summary: Adobe is enhancing the integration between Adobe Acrobat Sign and Adobe Experience Cloud to streamline digital document signing workflows for businesses. These advancements aim to improve efficiency, save time, and reduce costs. The new functionalities include faster e-signatures for work management, digitized commerce transactions, and personalized onboarding experiences.

title: Traction on Demand’s Greg Malpass on the Salesforce offer he couldn’t refuse
summary: Greg Malpass, founder and CEO of Traction on Demand, sold his company to Salesforce after 15 years, despite previously resisting acquisition offers. The deal, which has an undisclosed sale price but is expected to generate US$75 million in revenue for Salesforce in 2023, was an "incredible opportunity" that Malpass felt accountable to his employees to pursue. Traction on Demand, a Salesforce partner with 1,500 global employees, will become part of Salesforce Professional Services, with Malpass focused on a smooth integration before exploring his next steps within the larger company.

title: Autodesk Inc. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
summary: Autodesk Inc. (ADSK) stock fell 3.74% on Friday, closing at $192.31, underperforming the broader market as the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also declined. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for the company, with its stock trading significantly below its 52-week high. Competitors like Apple, Adobe, and Synopsys also experienced declines, while Autodesk's trading volume exceeded its 50-day average.

title: Autodesk Inc. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
summary: Autodesk Inc. (ADSK) stock dropped 3.74% on Friday, closing at $192.31, underperforming the broader market which also saw declines with the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average falling. This marks the second consecutive day of losses for Autodesk, leaving it significantly below its 52-week high. The stock's performance was also mixed compared to competitors like Apple, Adobe, and Synopsys, all of which also experienced declines.

title: Adobe: U.S. Consumers Spent $1.7 Trillion Online During the Pandemic, Rapidly Expanding the Digital Economy
summary: U.S. consumers spent $1.7 trillion online during the pandemic (March 2020 through February 2022), with online spending expected to surpass $1 trillion in 2022. Inflation accounted for $32 billion of this spend, while categories like groceries, electronics, and apparel saw significant shifts. Groceries, in particular, became a major e-commerce category, expected to exceed $85 billion in online sales in 2022.

title: Panera Bread drives highly personalized guest experiences with the Adobe Real-Time Customer Data Platform
summary: Panera Bread is enhancing personalized customer experiences across its omnichannel services by leveraging Adobe Real-Time Customer Data Platform (RTCDP). This integration allows Panera to gather real-time insights and orchestrate targeted campaigns, such as promoting soup options in colder climates or optimizing engagement for its Unlimited Sip Club. The company aims to meet high customer expectations for relevant, simple, and engaging interactions to drive loyalty and growth in the fast-casual industry.

title: SaaS customer relationship management (CRM) Market size to grow by USD 44.17 billion | Retail end-users to exhibit maximum demand | Technavio
summary: The SaaS Customer Relationship Management (CRM) market is projected to grow by USD 44.17 billion between 2020 and 2025, exhibiting a CAGR of 14%. Retail end-users are expected to drive the highest demand, significantly contributing to market growth due to the expansion of retail and e-commerce. North America is anticipated to dominate the market geographically, accounting for 52% of the global market expansion, primarily driven by the increasing adoption of cloud-based offerings and the need for customer information analysis.

title: Thursday's Top Analyst Upgrades and Downgrades: Adobe, American Airlines, Coupa Software, Disney, Host Hotels, Nio, Roblox, SentinelOne and More
summary: This article details top analyst upgrades, downgrades, and initiations for various companies on Thursday, March 17, 2022. It covers a range of sectors, providing updated price targets and ratings from major financial institutions like Stifel, Goldman Sachs, BofA Securities, Raymond James, Citigroup, Deutsche Bank, J.P. Morgan, Needham, Truist Securities, UBS, and Baird. The analysis follows a general market rally and the Federal Reserve's recent interest rate hike.

title: HubSpot Appoints Rob Giglio Chief Customer Officer
summary: HubSpot, a leading CRM platform, has appointed Rob Giglio as its new Chief Customer Officer. Giglio, who joins from DocuSign and previously spent 11 years at Adobe, will be responsible for overseeing HubSpot's flywheel organization and ensuring alignment across its marketing, sales, services, and revenue operations teams to enhance customer experience and drive strategic growth.

title: Intuit Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: Shares of Intuit Inc. (INTU) rallied 4.49% to $505.65 on Monday, outperforming competitors like Adobe Inc., SAP SE ADR, and Oracle Corp. The stock's performance contributed to an overall favorable trading session for the market, with the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also seeing gains. This marks the third consecutive day of gains for Intuit Inc., though it remains $211.21 short of its 52-week high.

title: Adobe Creative Cloud Experience makes it easier to run malware
summary: Adobe's Creative Cloud Experience, through a bundled Node.js executable, can be exploited to run unsigned code and potentially install malware, making it harder for threat detection systems to identify attacks. Security researchers have demonstrated this exploit, raising concerns among the security community and past Adobe customers who have reported suspicious activity related to the included `node.exe` file. Adobe has not yet responded to inquiries regarding this vulnerability.

title: Partner Innovation Spotlight: How Ginger Is Reimagining Mental Health Care in the Age of Digital
summary: This article features an interview with Karan Singh, founder of Ginger, discussing how his company is transforming mental health care through digital technology. Ginger, now part of Headspace Health, uses a smartphone-based platform to provide a full continuum of mental health services, including coaching, therapy, and psychiatry, aiming to remove barriers and destigmatize mental health support. The company emphasizes a team-based, personalized, and data-driven approach to care, with strong support from Cigna Ventures.

title: After Effects on Apple M1: giving motion designers the speed they need
summary: Adobe After Effects now offers native support for Apple M1, significantly boosting performance for motion designers, making it up to three times faster on M1 Ultra Macs. The latest release also introduces integrated review and approval tools with Frame.io for Creative Cloud and Scene Edit Detection, powered by Adobe Sensei, for more efficient workflow. These updates aim to enhance creative flexibility and speed up project turnaround times for users.

title: Autodesk Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: Autodesk Inc. stock (ADSK) fell 1.45% on Tuesday to $196.14, underperforming the broader market which also saw declines in the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. This marked the third consecutive day of losses for Autodesk, and its stock closed significantly below its 52-week high. The company showed mixed performance compared to competitors like Apple and Adobe.

title: What is Adobe Experience Cloud? CRM platform overview
summary: Adobe Experience Cloud is a comprehensive CRM platform designed for medium to large enterprises, offering tools to manage sales, marketing, and customer support. It facilitates the customer journey through data-driven insights with integrated features like targeted list generators, email campaigns, and real-time data updates. The platform also integrates with various third-party systems and social media, providing a unified view of customer interactions and supporting personalized digital experiences.

title: Pari Sawant has been appointed Chief Product Officer at Elavon, Inc.
summary: Pari Sawant has been appointed Chief Product Officer at Elavon, Inc., where he will oversee global product management with over 20 years of experience in consumer, SMB, and enterprise software. Sawant previously led the Document Cloud business and AI capabilities for Adobe.com, and held senior roles at Fiserv, MetricStream, eBay, and Hewlett-Packard. He holds an MBA from UC Berkeley and a Master's in Computer Science from SUNY Binghamton.

title: Fidelity Enters the Metaverse with New Financial Education Experience: Invest Quest at The Fidelity Stack
summary: Fidelity Investments has launched a new gamified financial education experience called "The Fidelity Stack" in Decentraland, an eight-story virtual building designed to teach users about ETF investing through an "Invest Quest" challenge. This initiative aims to engage younger audiences, particularly the 18-35 age range, following the company's recent introduction of a metaverse-themed ETF, FMET. Fidelity views this as a significant step in providing financial education in decentralized, virtual communities.

title: Here’s why TELUS International is a buy right now
summary: Greg Newman of Scotia Wealth Management recommends TELUS International (TSX:TIXT) as a buying opportunity, despite its recent dip alongside the broader software sector. He highlights the company's strong growth rate, its trading below issuance price, and attractive valuation at 19x its 2023 multiple. Newman suggests investors consider building a position gradually, expecting rewards over the next two to three years.

title: Dolby Laboratories Announces Dr. Shriram Revankar as SVP Advanced Technology Group
summary: Dolby Laboratories has appointed Dr. Shriram Revankar as its Senior Vice President of the Advanced Technology Group. Dr. Revankar, a seasoned technologist previously with Adobe and Xerox, will lead Dolby's global Advanced Technology Group to drive innovation. He brings extensive experience in identifying and delivering technological advancements for business growth, focusing on immersive experiences, AI, and computer vision.

title: Adobe Inc. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors
summary: Shares of Adobe Inc. (ADBE) fell 2.37% on Friday, closing at $391.01, amidst a generally negative trading session for the broader stock market. The stock underperformed several competitors, including Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) and Salesforce Inc. (CRM), which also saw declines. Adobe's stock ended the day significantly below its 52-week high.

title: Why Adobe Stock Rallied 4% Today
summary: Adobe stock rallied 4.3% today on a general rebound for beaten-down tech stocks, although the company's growth rate has slowed, and it's facing pressure from rising interest rates. Despite a 44% drop from all-time highs, Adobe is seen as a potentially undervalued long-term investment due to its leadership in cloud computing-based creative and document management tools. The company expects revenue growth to return to mid-teens percentages in its next quarter, though earnings per share growth may be slower due to geopolitical impacts and expansion investments.

title: Qualcomm Advances Development of Smarter and Safer Autonomous Robots for Logistics, Industry 4.0, and Urban Aerial Mobility with Next-Generation 5G and AI Robotics Solutions
summary: Qualcomm Technologies has unveiled an expanded roadmap of 5G and edge-AI robotics solutions, including the Qualcomm Robotics RB6 Platform and the Qualcomm RB5 AMR Reference Design. These new solutions aim to enhance autonomy and intelligence for various robotic applications in logistics, manufacturing, and urban air mobility by integrating advanced AI and 5G capabilities. The company is collaborating with multiple industry leaders to foster innovation in this rapidly evolving sector.

title: Adobe Digital Price Index: Online Inflation Slowing; Consumers Pull Back On Spending
summary: The Adobe Digital Price Index for April 2022 shows that online inflation is slowing, with prices increasing 2.9% year-over-year, down from 3.6% in March, and decreasing 0.5% month-over-month. Consumers spent $77.8 billion online in April, a decrease from the previous month, indicating a pullback in spending on durable goods. While some categories like electronics saw price drops, groceries and pet products continued to experience significant year-over-year increases.

title: 11 Newly Undervalued Wide-Moat Stocks
summary: This article identifies 11 high-quality, wide-moat stocks that have become undervalued due to recent market downturns, presenting opportunities for long-term investors. Companies like Adobe, Comcast, and MercadoLibre are highlighted as trading at steep discounts. The piece also details the competitive advantages ("moats") of several featured companies according to Morningstar analysts.

title: Oracle Corp. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: Oracle Corp. (ORCL) saw its stock rise 0.62% on Friday to $71.17, despite a generally positive day for the broader market with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also increasing. However, Oracle underperformed compared to some competitors and remained significantly below its 52-week high. Trading volume was also below its 50-day average.

title: The Newish Design Tool Giving Adobe a Run for the Money
summary: Figma, a design tool that debuted about five years ago, has quickly become a significant player in the design world, challenging Adobe's long-standing dominance. The author, initially skeptical, now champions Figma for its ease of use, browser-based accessibility, and open-source framework, making it beneficial for designers and non-designers alike. Figma's collaborative features and community-driven enhancements offer a powerful alternative for content creation, although it complements rather than fully replaces Adobe products for specialized tasks.

title: 7 Tips for Conducting Performance Reviews With Remote Employees
summary: This article provides seven tips for conducting effective performance reviews with remote employees, acknowledging the shift to widespread remote work. It covers strategies such as utilizing software for evaluations, conducting calibration sessions with managers, scheduling ample time for discussions, and ensuring a private, distraction-free environment. The guide emphasizes the importance of making remote reviews a permanent, smooth process for organizations adopting remote work by choice.

title: Citrix Systems Inc. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
summary: Shares of Citrix Systems Inc. slipped on Wednesday, but still outperformed major market indices and some competitors. The stock closed below its 52-week high, and trading volume was lower than its 50-day average.

title: ​Coincheck NFT（β版）、日本発ジェネラティブアートのNFTコレクション『Generativemasks』を5月26日より取扱い開始
summary: Coincheck NFT (β version) will begin handling the Japanese generative art NFT collection "Generativemasks" from May 26, 2022. On the same day, 100 pieces of "Generativemasks" will be sold on Coincheck NFT (β version) starting from 3 PM (scheduled). "Generativemasks," created by creative coder Shunsuke Takao, achieved over 300 million JPY in sales within two hours of its initial offering in August 2021.

title: Digital Marketing Software Market Giants Spending Is Going to Boom | Major Giants IBM, HubSpot, Microsoft
summary: A new study by HTF MI reveals that the worldwide digital marketing software market is expected to boom, with major players like IBM, HubSpot, and Microsoft driving growth. The research provides an in-depth analysis of market trends, drivers, and future prospects, covering segmentation by application (Large Enterprises & SMEs) and product type (On-Premises & Cloud). The report also highlights key market developments and includes a geographical analysis of the market.

title: Adobe’s Narayen Says Company ‘Always Looking’ for Acquisitions
summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen stated that the company is always seeking acquisition opportunities, emphasizing its strong cash reserves. Narayen outlined the criteria for potential deals, which include strong technology, cultural compatibility, and financial benefit for shareholders. This indicates Adobe's proactive strategy for growth through mergers and acquisitions.

title: Beware emailed PDFs containing Word docs exploiting old bugs
summary: HP cybersecurity researchers have discovered an email campaign distributing malware through PDFs that embed malicious Word documents, exploiting a four-year-old Microsoft Office vulnerability (CVE-2017-11882). This unusual method leverages user trust in PDF documents to deliver the Snake Keylogger, highlighting that old vulnerabilities remain effective and emphasizing the need for patching, user education, and robust email security.

title: Pinterest, Inc. Class A Common Stock (NYSE:PINS) Stock Quote
summary: This article provides a stock quote and financial details for Pinterest, Inc. Class A Common Stock (NYSE: PINS). It includes current price, volume, trading ranges, and a list of recent news headlines related to Pinterest and the broader tech market. The article also features a FAQ section answering common questions about Pinterest's public trading status and stock information.

title: Juniper Networks Case Study
summary: Juniper Networks successfully transformed its global website using Adobe Experience Manager, dramatically reducing content publication time by 50% and enabling personalized customer experiences. The company implemented a six-pillar strategy focusing on updated design, simplified content, SEO-friendliness, localized experiences, optimized measurement, and accelerated content creation. This digital transformation has led to significant improvements in agility, customer engagement, and operational efficiency, with plans for further enhancements like a guided buying experience.

title: How Adobe Acrobat Sign helps Acepodia comply with FDA regulations
summary: Acepodia, a biopharmaceutical company specializing in cell therapies for cancer, uses Adobe Acrobat Sign to digitize its agreements with research and academic organizations. This transition allows for faster signing processes, especially with remote employees and international collaborators, while ensuring compliance with FDA regulations like 21 CFR Part 11. The adoption of Adobe Acrobat Sign has reduced document completion time from weeks to hours and eliminated courier costs, freeing up time for critical research.

title: Optty and Payoneer Collaborate to Make Buy Now Pay Later Available to its Vast Merchant Base Around the Globe
summary: Optty and Payoneer have partnered to integrate Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) options into Payoneer's Payments Platform, making BNPL widely available to its global network of merchants. This collaboration allows B2B payment gateways and service providers to quickly offer various BNPL solutions, enhancing payment acceptance and customer choice across more than 59 markets. Optty's platform streamlines this integration, saving thousands of hours for providers like Payoneer, allowing retailers to easily activate BNPL options and accelerate sales.

title: Behold the bold — announcing the 2022 Adobe Experience Maker Awards Finalists
summary: Adobe has announced the finalists for the 2022 Adobe Experience Maker Awards, celebrating companies and individuals who have demonstrated innovative and impactful achievements in customer experience using Adobe Experience Cloud. The awards recognize leaders across global and regional categories for their ability to deliver personalization at scale, create seamless customer journeys, and drive business growth. Winners will be revealed during virtual gala events on June 22nd and 23rd.

title: EPAM Named an Adobe Platinum Level Solution Partner
summary: EPAM Systems, Inc. has achieved platinum status within the Adobe Solution Partner Program, placing it in the top 15 percent of solution providers. This recognition highlights EPAM's exceptional customer satisfaction, successful Adobe Experience Cloud deployments, and over 300 credentialed Adobe specialists. The company emphasizes its dedication to the Adobe ecosystem, delivering innovative, omni-channel solutions for digital transformation.

title: How to patent your idea
summary: This article from Adobe Blog discusses the importance of patents, especially in creative industries, and provides insights from Adobe's "patent centurions" on how to successfully obtain one. It emphasizes the need for creativity, novelty, and a thorough application process, warning against attempting it alone. The piece highlights that patents not only protect intellectual property but also drive further funding for R&D, creating a cycle of innovation and progress.

title: 3 keys to a successful marketing system of record
summary: A marketing system of record acts as a centralized operational and informational tool for modern marketing efforts. It requires a clear goal, the ability to inspire questions that address information gaps, and leveraging cohesive strategic data sources. Adobe Workfront is presented as a solution that helps organizations achieve these goals for better marketing coordination and decision-making.

title: Adobe Photoshop On the Web Will be Free to Everyone
summary: Adobe is making a basic web-based version of Photoshop free to all users, currently being trialed in Canada. This move aims to expand Photoshop's user base by offering core features to everyday consumers and students, particularly those using less capable devices like Chromebooks. While it provides a simplified experience, Adobe hopes it will encourage users to eventually subscribe to the full desktop version.

title: Next-Generation Adobe Analytics Delivers Customer Insights From Streaming Media and the Metaverse
summary: Adobe has launched new services for Adobe Analytics, creating a unified workspace for brands to gather data and insights from various media, including the metaverse and streaming content. This update allows for seamless data migration from other analytics platforms, ensuring compliance with regulations like GDPR and CCPA. By supporting emerging channels, Adobe Analytics aims to provide comprehensive omnichannel analysis through Customer Journey Analytics, helping businesses understand evolving consumer behaviors and deliver personalized experiences.

title: Edmunds sees near 100% completion with Adobe Learning Manager
summary: Edmunds leverages Adobe Learning Manager to train its mobile sales force, achieving a near 100% course completion rate. The platform enables quick content creation, supports video learning across various devices, and integrates with Salesforce, allowing Edmunds to efficiently deliver expertise to dealerships nationwide. This strategic move helps Edmunds maintain its competitive edge by keeping its sales team updated on automotive trends and marketing tools.

title: Joining a Tech Icon: Adobe CFO Dan Durn
summary: This article features an interview with Adobe CFO Dan Durn, who discusses his transition from the semiconductor industry to Adobe in October 2021. Durn highlights Adobe's position at the center of digital content and data, its successful shift to cloud-based solutions, and the company's innovation engine. He also touches on plans for the finance department, including integrating IT and a high bar for future M&A activities.

title: Stanley Black & Decker unites work tools via Adobe Workfront
summary: Stanley Black & Decker leveraged Adobe Workfront to streamline its work processes, eliminate redundant tasks, and enhance collaboration across ten teams. The integration of Workfront with existing platforms like Avanti Slingshot automated previously manual data entries and updates, significantly improving efficiency and productivity. This strategic move allows employees to focus on value-added work, while gaining better control and visibility over reports and project tracking.

title: Adobe Acrobat Blocking 30 Security Apps From Scanning PDFs
summary: Adobe Acrobat Reader has been found to actively block 30 different security applications from scanning PDF files by preventing DLL injection into its processes. Adobe claims this is due to incompatibility with their Chromium-based engine (CEF), but other programs using CEF do not exhibit this behavior. This issue raises concerns about security vulnerabilities in PDFs, prompting some to suggest finding alternative PDF readers while Adobe works on a solution.

title: PGY Stock Price, Forecast & Analysis | PAGAYA TECHNOLOGIES LTD -A (NASDAQ:PGY)
summary: This article provides a detailed analysis of Pagaya Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ: PGY) stock, covering its price performance, technical and fundamental analysis, analyst ratings, and financial highlights. As of February 20, 2026, the stock price is $11.89, and analysts project a significant price increase of 244% in the next year. The company, which specializes in AI and data networks for the financial industry, has an average fundamental rating but shows concerns regarding profitability.

title: USD 542.87 million Growth in Photo Editing Software Market | Dominant Players Including CD Systems International Inc., Adobe Inc., Afterlight Collective Inc., Alphabet Inc., and Apple Inc. among others | Technavio
summary: The photo editing software market is projected to grow by USD 542.87 million between 2021 and 2026, accelerating at a CAGR of 9.27%. Key drivers include increasing smartphone penetration and growth in web design services, while challenges include the availability of free software. North America is expected to contribute 37% to this growth, with the commercial end-user segment dominating the market.

title: Announcement: Adobe Flash (End-of-Life) Removal
summary: Chapman University's Information Systems & Technology (IS&T) announced the removal of Adobe Flash from all Chapman-owned devices on August 3, 2022, due to Adobe discontinuing support and security updates for the software since December 31, 2020. This measure is crucial for protecting university systems from potential vulnerabilities. The IT department also recommended users uninstall Flash from personal computers and provided resources for assistance.

title: Adobe plans to move into its new San Jose tower early next year
summary: Adobe is set to occupy its new North Tower in downtown San Jose in early 2023, significantly expanding its headquarters. This fourth building will almost double the company's headcount capacity in the city. The move signifies Adobe's continued growth and investment in its San Jose operations.

title: Adobe North Tower Facade Complete, Move-ins Expected by Early 2023, San Jose
summary: The facade of Adobe North Tower in San Jose has been fully installed, with move-ins anticipated by early 2023. The 18-story building at 333 West San Fernando Street, designed by Gensler and constructed by Devcon, will add 690,330 square feet of R&D offices to Adobe's campus. It will be an all-electric structure with various amenities and excellent transit access.

title: Artificial Intelligence at Nvidia – Two Current Use Cases
summary: This article examines two current use cases of Artificial Intelligence at Nvidia. The first use case details how Nvidia uses machine learning to enhance video resolution and audio quality during real-time video calls. The second use case explains how Nvidia, in collaboration with Adobe, uses AI algorithms to analyze marketing campaign effectiveness and optimize sales for a new GPU line.

title: Meet our experts
summary: This article introduces devmio's panel of tech experts, who are renowned thought leaders and practitioners in various fields like JavaScript, DevOps, AI, and API development. It highlights their role in sharing knowledge through conferences, articles, and live events, and invites other experts to join their diverse community. The platform emphasizes inclusivity and offers benefits such as full content access and networking opportunities for its experts.

title: Why Adobe Stock Is Down 35% So Far in 2022
summary: Adobe's stock has dropped 35% in the first half of 2022 due to a slowing growth pace, despite strong sales and cash flow. Investors are concerned about a "growth hangover" as work patterns normalize post-pandemic, leading to a conservative outlook from management for the latter half of the year. While the company still expects strong sales for 2022, the short-term market focus on decelerating demand is pressuring the stock.

title: Authorities announce plans for site of soon-to-be former Utah prison
summary: Authorities have revealed plans for "The Point," the 600-acre site of Utah's former correctional facility in Draper. This development aims to create Utah's Innovation Community, situated within the rapidly growing tech corridor. The project includes plans for 3,000 housing units in phase one, with 12.5% designated as affordable housing, addressing concerns about housing costs and water resources in the region.

title: Making the health insurance document process hassle-free for customers
summary: This article discusses how health insurance companies can improve the document processing experience for customers and internal teams by leveraging cloud-based solutions like Adobe Document Cloud and its integration with Microsoft offerings. It highlights the benefits of digitizing workflows, such as increased efficiency, faster turnaround times, cost savings, and a better overall customer experience. The piece also provides examples of how a Fortune 500 healthcare insurer utilizes these integrated solutions for various internal and external processes.

title: Stock-Split Watch: Is Adobe Next?
summary: Adobe could be the next high-profile company to announce a stock split, following a trend set by others like Alphabet and Amazon in 2022. While stock splits don't alter a company's fundamental valuation, they can make shares more accessible to retail investors and help companies attract/retain talent. Adobe has a history of 2-for-1 splits, with its last one occurring 17 years ago, and its strong financial performance in Q2 2022 suggests a healthy business that could support such a move.

title: LinkedIn Still Number One Brand to be Faked in Phishing Attempts while Microsoft Surges up the Rankings to Number Two Spot in Q2 Report
summary: Check Point Research's Q2 Brand Phishing Report reveals that LinkedIn remains the most imitated brand in phishing attempts, accounting for 45% of all such attacks. Microsoft has significantly risen to the second spot with 13%, surpassing DHL, while new brands like Adidas, Adobe, and HSBC have entered the top 10. The report highlights the dangers of these scams, with examples provided for LinkedIn, DHL, Outlook, and Amazon phishing attempts.

title: AiThority Interview with Julie Lock, Senior Manager, UK&I Marketing at HubSpot
summary: Julie Lock, Senior Manager, UK&I Marketing at HubSpot, discusses her role, HubSpot's customer-centric approach, and the evolution of CRMs. She highlights how HubSpot's platform differs from competitors and addresses key challenges CRMs solve for different departments. Lock also shares insights on the integration of AI with CRMs, the relationship between CRMs and CDPs, and market differences between the UK and North America, all while emphasizing the importance of solving for the customer in a strong economic climate.

title: Yelp's departure is a blow for city's office market
summary: Yelp's decision to close its New York City offices and embrace a fully remote workforce is a significant blow to the city's office market, particularly for landlords in downtown Manhattan. The departure of Yelp from its 200,000 square feet at 11 Madison Square, along with other tech company retrenchments, highlights a trend of vacated spaces not being refilled, impacting the commercial real estate sector. This move reflects a broader shift by tech firms towards remote work models, leaving landlords concerned about the future of office occupancy.

title: Yamini Rangan of HubSpot tops Comparably’s Best CEOs for Women 2022
summary: Yamini Rangan of HubSpot has been recognized as the top CEO on Comparably's 5th Annual Best CEOs for Women list, based on sentiment ratings from female employees. This year's list includes 17 Indian American CEOs across both large and small companies, highlighting their leadership in fostering diverse and inclusive workplace cultures. Comparably determines these rankings based on positive sentiment ratings and employee participation, without fees or nominations.

title: How Humor and Hormones Come Alive in Netflix’s Big Mouth and Human Resources
summary: This article features an interview with editor Felipe Salazar about his work on Netflix's animated series "Big Mouth" and its spinoff "Human Resources." Salazar discusses his editing process using Adobe Premiere Pro, his favorite scenes, challenges faced during production (especially during the pandemic), and his creative inspirations. He highlights the importance of standardized project templates and tools like Frame.io and offers advice for aspiring filmmakers.

title: Compare top email marketing services
summary: This article reviews 14 top email marketing services, including Adobe Campaign, Mailchimp, Constant Contact, and HubSpot, detailing their key features and ideal use cases. It aims to help businesses of all sizes choose the most suitable platform based on their specific needs, subscriber list size, and marketing strategy. The article also provides guidance on important factors to consider when selecting an email marketing service.

title: Gartner Magic Quadrant for Personalization Engines 2022
summary: This article examines the 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Personalization Engines, categorizing vendors into Leaders, Challengers, Visionaries, and Niche Players. It details Gartner's definition of personalization engines and highlights the strengths and cautions of 13 prominent providers like Dynamic Yield, Insider, Salesforce, Adobe, and SAP. The report aims to help businesses understand the market and choose suitable personalization solutions.

title: Announcing the 2022 Adobe Analytics Champions
summary: Adobe announced its 2022 Adobe Analytics Champions, recognizing 40 top advocates globally for their expertise and passion. These champions, selected from hundreds of applications, excel in helping fellow users improve skills and product knowledge. The program offers career development, speaking engagements, and opportunities to provide feedback to Adobe product teams.

title: Gartner CFO Survey Reveals Real Estate and Finance Functions Most Likely to Face Cuts Within a Year
summary: A July 2022 Gartner survey of over 200 CFOs indicates that real estate/facilities management and finance functions are most vulnerable to budget cuts in the next year due to factors like increased remote work. Conversely, IT and sales functions are expected to see spending increases, with 40% of CFOs planning to boost IT investment, reflecting a focus on digital technology and automation to combat inflation and drive growth. The report suggests that "efficient growth" companies, which strategically invest during economic difficulties, achieve better long-term results.

title: Salesforce Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: Salesforce Inc. (CRM) shares fell 3.96% on Tuesday, closing at $182.24, underperforming the broader market as the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also declined. This marked the third consecutive day of losses for the stock, placing it significantly below its 52-week high. Salesforce also lagged behind some competitors, with Microsoft Corp. rising while Adobe Inc. and SAP SE ADR experienced smaller declines.

title: Canadian University Dubai first private university in UAE to partner with Coursera to prepare students for in-demand jobs
summary: Canadian University Dubai (CUD) has partnered with Coursera to integrate its Career Academy into CUD's curriculum, making it the first private university in the UAE to do so. This collaboration will equip students with in-demand skills and industry-recognized Professional Certificates from leading companies like Google, IBM, and Meta, at no additional cost during their final year. The initiative aims to enhance graduates' employability and provide a competitive edge in the rapidly evolving digital job market.

title: The Home Depot unifies customer experiences with Adobe
summary: The Home Depot has successfully unified its online and in-store customer experiences using Adobe products, leading to a 62% increase in personalized campaigns and a 14% rise in net sales year over year. The company leverages Adobe Real-Time Customer Data Platform and Adobe Experience Platform to create unified customer profiles, enabling highly relevant messaging and inspiration for customers' home improvement projects. This data-driven approach, along with innovative tools like a mobile app for in-store navigation and AI-powered product search, allows The Home Depot to cater to diverse customer journeys, from project-based customers to item buyers and contractors.

title: Dick’s attacks cart abandonment with predictive AI
summary: Dick's Sporting Goods is implementing AI-based software from Metrical to combat cart abandonment by dynamically targeting individual online shopper needs in real time. The retailer aims to leverage predictive AI to personalize content and improve customer experience, ultimately leading to higher conversion rates and increased customer lifetime value. This initiative builds on Dick's broader strategy to establish a robust data backbone for personalized marketing, utilizing platforms like Adobe Experience Cloud and Adobe Real-Time Customer Data Platform.

title: What’s Changed: 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Multichannel Marketing Hubs
summary: This article analyzes the 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Multichannel Marketing Hubs, detailing the evolving market criteria and the strengths of 17 significant providers. It categorizes vendors into Leaders, Challengers, Niche Players, and Visionaries, highlighting Adobe, Salesforce, and Oracle among others. The report also notes changes like SAP Marketing Cloud being replaced by SAP Emarsys and the addition of MoEngage.

title: Trajector Disability helps clients get faster, more secure access to vital government benefits using Adobe Acrobat Sign
summary: Trajector Disability, which helps individuals access Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) benefits, has significantly improved its client engagement and application processing by adopting Adobe Acrobat Sign. This change has streamlined the application process, reducing the time it takes for clients to complete and return necessary paperwork from months to minutes, thereby accelerating access to vital benefits. The integration of e-signatures has also enhanced security, accountability, and overall efficiency for both clients and the company.

title: Illumina wins case against FTC in bid to hold onto early cancer detection company Grail
summary: Illumina has won its case against the Federal Trade Commission, allowing it to retain Grail, an early cancer detection company it acquired for $8 billion. An administrative law judge rejected the FTC's argument that the acquisition would stifle competition in the multi-cancer early detection market. This decision is seen by Illumina's CEO as a step toward making Grail's life-saving Galleri test widely accessible and affordable.

title: Stagnant Sales Are Creating an Inventory Glut
summary: Global retail volatility over the past 18 months has led to an inventory glut due to resolving supply chain issues and declining consumer demand, particularly for non-essential goods. This situation forces retailers to seek additional storage or deeply discount products, benefiting liquidation companies. The article highlights shifting consumer spending habits towards essentials and the impact on major retailers.

title: Western Digital, Epic Games Release Fortnite SanDisk microSDXC Card for Nintendo Switch
summary: Western Digital, Nintendo, and Epic Games have collaborated to release an officially licensed Fortnite SanDisk microSDXC card for Nintendo Switch owners. These cards feature Fortnite characters, come in 128GB and 256GB capacities, and include a digital access code for an exclusive in-game item. The partnership aims to enhance the gaming experience for Fortnite fans by providing additional storage and themed accessories.

title: Adobe shares plunge on deal to acquire design platform Figma for $20 billion
summary: Adobe announced its acquisition of design software firm Figma for approximately $20 billion in cash and stock, causing Adobe's shares to plunge 17% — its biggest drop since 2010. Figma, a cloud-based design software company valued at $10 billion in 2021, is expected to generate over $400 million in annual recurring revenue this year. The acquisition comes as Adobe reported mixed fiscal third-quarter results and issued conservative guidance for the fourth quarter.

title: Adobe Tumbles After Deal to Buy Figma for About $20 Billion
summary: Adobe Inc. announced an agreement to acquire software design startup Figma Inc. for approximately $20 billion, a deal comprising half cash and half stock. This acquisition, Adobe's largest ever, sent its shares to their sharpest single-day decline since 2010 due to market concerns about the high price. The move aims to broaden Adobe's creative tool offerings for professionals.

title: Adobe's $20 billion deal to buy Figma is a pricey bet on collaboration
summary: Adobe has agreed to acquire Figma, a collaborative design platform and competitor, for approximately $20 billion in cash and stock. This acquisition, Adobe's largest, aims to integrate collaboration tools essential for remote work and expand Adobe's market opportunities, despite skepticism from analysts regarding the high valuation and the deal's dilutive effect on Adobe's Non-GAAP EPS for the first two years. Figma's CEO, Dylan Field, stated that the company will maintain its pricing and incorporate Adobe's creative expertise, reporting to Adobe's president of digital media.

title: Adobe's $20B Figma deal ranks as company's largest acquisition
summary: Adobe Inc. is set to acquire online design platform Figma Inc. in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $20 billion, making it Adobe's largest acquisition to date. This move comes as Adobe reported fiscal fourth-quarter revenue guidance below Wall Street expectations, despite achieving 15% year-over-year revenue growth in Q3. Figma will continue to operate autonomously under Adobe, aiming to accelerate the web-based delivery of Creative Cloud technologies.

title: Figma founder Dylan Field, a college dropout, to get $2 bn after selling startup to Adobe
summary: Figma founder Dylan Field, a college dropout, is set to receive a stake worth over $2 billion after his company was acquired by Adobe in an approximately $20 billion cash-and-stock deal. Field will continue to lead Figma, which will operate independently until the deal closes in 2023, subject to regulatory approvals. This acquisition marks a significant win for Figma's venture capital backers and is expected to be accretive to Adobe's earnings within three years.

title: Quiet quitting energized by employee fearlessness
summary: Quiet quitting, characterized by employees doing only what is required, is being fueled by a strong job market and a lack of fear among employees regarding job loss. This trend, while a new term for an old problem ("quitting in place"), has broadened since COVID-19, leading employers to reconsider how they manage and reward their workforce. Companies are exploring "stay interviews," training managers, and adopting employee monitoring technology to address this societal shift.

title: S|W: The SaaS Weekly – Adobe has $20 billion designs on Figma
summary: This edition of S|W: The SaaS Weekly highlights Adobe's significant $20 billion acquisition of Figma and Google facing a $25 billion lawsuit. The newsletter also covers various funding rounds for Canadian startups like GrowerIQ and Bright, leadership changes at Thinkific, and financial updates from companies like D2L and Byju's, alongside news of layoffs at GoStudent and Twilio.

title: Getty and Shutterstock are the latest platforms to ban AI-generated images
summary: Getty Images and Shutterstock have become the latest platforms to ban AI-generated imagery due to concerns over copyright, unclear rights, and potential legal repercussions. Getty's CEO, Craig Peters, emphasized the need to protect the company and its customers from disputes arising from AI tools that often rely on copyrighted works without attribution or compensation. While Getty explicitly prohibits AI-generated content, Shutterstock is also removing such images, significantly reducing their presence on the platform.

title: Adobe Outlines Figma Feature Ideas, Commits to Keeping Free Tier
summary: Adobe Inc. announced plans to integrate its creative software technology into Figma following its acquisition, promising to maintain Figma's current pricing and simplicity, including its freemium model. Adobe's Chief Product Officer, Scott Belsky, stated that features like photo, video, and illustration editing, along with linking projects from Adobe products, will be added while ensuring user experience remains uncluttered. This aims to alleviate concerns among loyal Figma users about potential changes to the design app.

title: Salesforce Inc. stock rises Wednesday, still underperforms market
summary: Salesforce Inc. stock rose 0.86% on Wednesday to $150.17, despite an overall positive day for the stock market where the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also saw gains. However, the company's stock still underperformed several key competitors, including Microsoft, Adobe, and SAP SE. Salesforce Inc. remains significantly below its 52-week high.

title: ARK Invest CEO Cathie Wood’s tech bargain hunt
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest continued its strategy of investing in tech stocks in September 2022, acquiring shares in companies like Nvidia and Adobe despite significant market downturns for these firms. The ARK Innovation ETF has seen substantial losses and outflows, but the firm is betting on a rebound in battered tech stocks. Additionally, there has been a management reshuffle within ARK Invest, with William Scherer taking over two ETFs and Brett Winton promoted to chief futurist.

title: Media Alert: Adobe Unveils Photoshop Elements and Premiere Elements 2023 with Faster, Easier Photo and Video Editing
summary: Adobe has released Photoshop Elements 2023 and Premiere Elements 2023, featuring new AI-powered tools for easier photo and video editing. The updates include enhanced animation, artistic effects, 100 new audio tracks for videos, and faster application performance. Additionally, new web and mobile companion apps (English-only beta) offer convenient on-the-go access, viewing, and sharing of creative content.

title: Japan’s Executive of the Year Shinya Suda: “Don’t Hesitate to Share Your Ambition”
summary: Shinya Suda, Senior Vice President of Information Systems at Astellas Pharma Inc., was named Japan’s Executive of the Year in the 2022 Adobe Experience Maker Awards. A certified pharmacist, Suda was recognized for his three-decade career in IT, specifically for leading a company-wide effort to enhance customer experiences and standardize the organization's global technology stack using Adobe Experience Cloud solutions. He shared insights into his career journey, the digital transformation at Astellas Pharma, and his advice for other leaders to boldly share their ambitions.

title: Adobe Stock Has Been Beaten Down. Cathie Wood’s ARK Buys the Slide.
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Next Generation Internet ETF recently purchased approximately $6.8 million worth of Adobe shares. This acquisition follows a decline in Adobe's stock price, which occurred after the software maker announced its acquisition of rival company Figma.

title: Walmart’s fulfillment tech expands to Asda stores
summary: Asda, the U.K. grocery chain, will implement Walmart's "Store Assist" app in over 330 of its superstores to enhance in-store picking efficiency. This move is part of Walmart Commerce Technologies' strategy to expand its presence as a technology vendor. The app, built on Walmart's existing fulfillment technology, aims to improve accuracy and speed in omnichannel fulfillment.

title: Media Alert: Adobe Delivers Powerful New Creative, Document and Marketing Capabilities for SMBs
summary: Adobe announced new automation and collaboration features across Adobe Creative Cloud and the Adobe product portfolio, aimed at helping small and mid-sized businesses (SMBs) modernize workflows and enhance productivity. The company also unveiled new partnerships with Mastercard, Etsy, and Meta to extend access to Adobe Creative Cloud and Adobe Document Cloud for SMBs. These innovations and partnerships address the growing reliance of SMBs on digital solutions amid labor shortages and increasing uncertainty, providing tools to simplify tasks, improve collaboration, and automate marketing efforts.

title: Adobe Introduces New Collaboration Capabilities In Creative Cloud and Document Cloud
summary: Adobe announced new collaboration tools within Creative Cloud and Document Cloud at Adobe MAX, enhancing workflows for creators. These updates include Share for Review (beta) for Photoshop and Illustrator, new Camera to Cloud integrations with RED and Fujifilm, and improved PDF collaboration features for Acrobat. The enhancements aim to streamline content creation, review processes, and document management for distributed teams.

title: Adobe Unveils Powerful New Collaboration and AI Capabilities in Photoshop
summary: Adobe showcased new collaboration and AI features for Photoshop at Adobe MAX. Key updates include Share for Review for simplified collaboration and AI-powered enhancements like selection improvements, a one-click delete and fill tool, and photo restoration neural filters for Photoshop on the web. These innovations aim to make Photoshop smarter, more intuitive, and accessible across various devices, including desktop, iPad, and web browsers.

title: Adobe Stock and Substance Are Now Included in the Creative Cloud Suite
summary: Chapman University, an Adobe Creative Campus, has integrated Adobe Stock and Adobe Substance into its Creative Cloud Suite for its community. Adobe Stock provides access to over 600,000 high-quality images directly within Adobe applications, serving as an alternative to services like Unsplash. The Adobe Substance suite includes four design apps for 3D asset creation and texturing, enhancing digital skills for students and faculty.

title: HUGO BOSS Partners With Adobe To Drive 3D Innovation in Fashion
summary: HUGO BOSS is partnering with Adobe to implement 3D innovation in its fashion design processes, utilizing Adobe Substance 3D applications. This collaboration aims to empower designers to create inspiring and sustainable fashion, while also supporting the brands' ambitions in immersive 3D experiences and the metaverse. The adoption of 3D design helps HUGO BOSS streamline its design workflows, engage suppliers, and explore new customer experiences, contributing to sustainability efforts by reducing the need for physical samples and travel for content production.

title: Salesforce Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: Salesforce Inc. (CRM) stock rallied 2.49% to $157.50 on a strong trading day, outperforming the broader market which saw the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average decline. This marked the stock's fourth consecutive day of gains, though it remained significantly below its 52-week high. Salesforce also outperformed several key competitors including Microsoft Corp. and SAP SE ADR.

title: Digitizing CX and employee workflows drives 205% ROI for government
summary: A Forrester Total Economic Impact (TEI) study, commissioned by Adobe, revealed that digitizing customer experience (CX) and employee workflows with Adobe Document Cloud and Adobe Experience Cloud can drive a 205% ROI for government agencies, with payback in just six months. The study highlights how these tools help agencies meet federal mandates to modernize service delivery, reduce paperwork burdens, and streamline operations, leading to significant savings in IT costs, customer service interactions, and labor hours. By migrating to the cloud, digitizing forms, and improving website functionality, government organizations can enhance productivity, improve citizen-facing services, and achieve substantial cost efficiencies.

title: Predictions 2023: AI Will Become An Indispensable, Trusted Enterprise Coworker
summary: In 2023, AI technology is predicted to become an indispensable and trusted enterprise coworker, transforming global markets. Key predictions include TuringBots writing 10% of worldwide code, one in four tech executives reporting to their board on AI governance, and 10% of Fortune 500 enterprises generating content with AI tools. These advancements signify AI's foundational role in successful enterprises and continued investment.

title: Fingerprinting Web Applications and APIs using Qualys Web Application Scanning
summary: This article explains how Qualys Web Application Scanning (WAS) uses fingerprinting to identify underlying components of web applications and APIs, enhancing an organization's ability to detect and reduce attack vectors. It details the WAS approach, including its ability to detect over 120 technologies and common misconfigurations through QID 150247. The piece also provides guidance on configuring and interpreting WAS scan reports to protect infrastructure from attackers.

title: Why Figma is selling to Adobe for $20 billion, with CEO Dylan Field
summary: Dylan Field, CEO of Figma, discusses the company's $20 billion sale to Adobe, addressing concerns about competition and Figma's independence within the larger company structure. He emphasizes the benefits of integration with Adobe's ecosystem for improved design workflows and touches upon Figma's roots as a web application built on WebGL, along with his thoughts on the future of VR and AI. Field also reveals how the acquisition announcement led to a surprising increase in job applications, challenging the typical "golden handcuffs" narrative.

title: Fast-growing Amaze acquires Spring in landmark deal to accelerate creator commerce
summary: Amaze Software Inc. has acquired Spring's merchandising platform to bolster its offerings for the creator economy. This acquisition brings 5.5 million new creator stores to Amaze, along with leadership experience from major tech companies, aiming to provide enhanced tools for creators to monetize their content and engage authentically with customers. Aaron Day, CEO of Amaze, emphasized that the deal will accelerate Amaze's ability to support creator success and expand social commerce, merchandising, and marketing capabilities.

title: AI-powered video and audio editing app Descript raises $50M
summary: Descript Inc., an AI-powered video and audio editing platform, has raised a $50 million Series C funding round led by the OpenAI Startup Fund, bringing its total raised to over $100 million. Founded by former Groupon CEO Andrew Mason, the app simplifies editing for podcasters and videographers using AI and has launched new features, including background removal and scene creation, to make video editing as intuitive as using a Word document. OpenAI's COO Brad Lightcap expressed excitement for tools like Descript that empower creators.

title: Qualcomm and Adobe Unleash Creativity Across Snapdragon Mobile, Compute and XR Devices
summary: Qualcomm and Adobe have announced an expanded collaboration to enhance creative experiences on Snapdragon-powered devices across mobile, compute, and XR platforms. They aim to bring Adobe Creative Cloud products, including Photoshop, Lightroom, Fresco, and Acrobat, natively to Snapdragon, leveraging Adobe Sensei AI and Qualcomm AI Engine for improved performance in creative workflows. The companies are also working on XR-driven authoring and consumption workflows using the Snapdragon Spaces XR Developer Platform to extend Adobe's 3D and Immersive tools to VR/MR headsets and AR glasses.

title: Stock Split Watch: Is Adobe Next?
summary: This article discusses the possibility of Adobe undertaking a stock split, noting that it hasn't had one in 17 years. While splits don't change company fundamentals, they can increase retail investor interest. The author believes Adobe is a great long-term investment but doesn't anticipate a stock split happening soon, especially given current market conditions and the need for its stock price to rally above $500 to create pressure for a split.

title: Pegasystems updates CRM, RPA and low-code with Pega Infinity 8.8
summary: Pegasystems has released Pega Infinity 8.8, bringing significant updates to its CRM, RPA, and low-code solutions. Key enhancements include an AI-powered Developer Assistant Panel for Pega Platform, improved tools for Robotic Automation, and new pre-built data integrations for Pega Customer Decision Hub. The update also introduces 'call-wrapping' features in Pega Customer Service using AI transcription and enhanced integration between Customer Service and Customer Decision Hub.

title: Black Friday on track to top $9 billion in online sales for first time
summary: Online sales for Black Friday were expected to reach a record $9 billion, potentially hitting $9.2 billion, despite recession worries and inflation. Adobe Analytics reported $7.28 billion in sales by 6 p.m. Eastern, with popular items including Apple smartwatches, AirPods, Funko Pop! figurines, and Pokémon videogames. This marks the first time Black Friday online sales were projected to exceed $9 billion.

title: Black Friday is here, inflation included
summary: This Black Friday, shoppers returned to familiar holiday patterns but were significantly impacted by inflation, leading to more selective spending habits and a reliance on sales, savings, or "buy now, pay later" services. While store traffic increased compared to the previous pandemic-affected years, overall sales growth is expected to slow down, and many consumers are delaying purchases to find better deals. The article highlights how inflation for essential goods is reshaping holiday spending behaviors, pushing more shoppers to look for bargains and use credit more broadly.

title: U.S. Black Friday online sales hit record $9 bln despite high inflation- Adobe Analytics
summary: U.S. shoppers spent a record $9.12 billion online this Black Friday, marking a 2.3% increase despite high inflation, according to Adobe Analytics. Consumers sought steep discounts on various items, holding out for big shopping days. Adobe forecasts Cyber Monday will be the largest online shopping day, generating $11.2 billion in sales, with mobile shopping accounting for 48% of all Black Friday digital sales.

title: USMC Extends Elite Training to the Digital Classroom
summary: The United States Marine Corps has transformed its eLearning platform, MarineNet®, using Adobe solutions to provide 24/7 access to digital courses, rich training videos, and collaborative tools for over 300,000 members. This initiative, driven by the Marine Corps Education Command (EDCOM) and the College of Distance Education and Training (CDET), has standardized online content, enabled user-generated media, and reduced the annual cost of eLearning per user by 38%, while also ensuring accessibility on any device and adherence to Department of Defense security standards. The move was accelerated by the COVID-19 pandemic, highlighting the need for on-demand, virtual training.

title: Recosoft announces DecksMoveIn - Import PowerPoint® into Adobe® InDesign®
summary: Recosoft Corporation has announced DecksMoveIn, a new plug-in for Adobe InDesign that allows direct conversion of PowerPoint (.pptx) files into InDesign (.indd) format. This tool aims to streamline the workflow for creative professionals by converting text, images, graphics, and slide masters while preserving layout, eliminating the need for manual copy-pasting or other time-consuming workarounds. DecksMoveIn is compatible with both macOS and Windows platforms and will be part of the PDF2ID Suite 2023 edition.

title: Salesforce executive exits help push stock to its lowest point since March 2020
summary: Salesforce's stock has hit its lowest point since March 2020 following the departure of Slack CEO Stewart Butterfield and co-CEO Bret Taylor. The company's shares have dropped 47% this year, facing investor concerns over executive turbulence and slowing revenue growth. Analysts have noted the lack of future guidance and significant leadership changes as "two elephants in the room," contributing to a negative outlook.

title: Adobe Cuts 100 Jobs Concentrated in Sales as Tech Tightens Belt
summary: Adobe Inc. has eliminated approximately 100 jobs, primarily in sales, as part of a broader trend of tech companies reducing expenses through staff cuts. While some affected employees were offered alternative roles within the company, Adobe's workforce reduction is significantly smaller compared to the thousands of jobs cut by other tech giants like Amazon and Meta. The San Jose-based software company had over 28,700 employees by the end of its fiscal third quarter.

title: Adobe Eliminates 100 Jobs in Sales, Joins Other Companies in Using Job Cuts to Reduce Expenses
summary: Adobe Inc. has eliminated 100 jobs in its sales division, aligning with other tech companies reducing expenses through workforce adjustments. Despite these cuts, Adobe stated it is not undergoing company-wide layoffs and is still hiring for critical roles, reassigning some affected employees to support "critical initiatives." The company's adjustments are significantly smaller compared to thousands of job cuts reported by other tech giants this year.

title: Jessica Criscione transforms the martech stack at IBM
summary: Jessica Criscione, CTO of marketing and sales intelligence at IBM, spearheaded a significant transformation of IBM's marketing technology stack, consolidating over 40 systems into five, with Adobe Experience Cloud at its core. This initiative resulted in $120 million in savings, improved email response rates, and faster content delivery, demonstrating the power of bold change management. Criscione emphasized the importance of unified strategy, cross-team communication, and embracing "radical self-responsibility" and imperfection for success in large-scale digital transformations.

title: Building the future of retail with Adobe and Dynamics 365
summary: Microsoft is revamping its online store on microsoft.com by integrating Adobe Experience Cloud, Microsoft Dynamics 365, and Microsoft Azure. This initiative aims to improve content management efficiency for developers and create a more accessible and intuitive interface for customers. The shift replaces an internal custom solution with a suite of Adobe products linked to Dynamics 365 Commerce, hosted on Azure, to enhance scalability and adaptability for Microsoft Store's digital storefronts.

title: Coke CMO Melanie Boulden Moves to Tyson Foods
summary: Melanie Boulden, formerly North America CMO for Coca-Cola, is moving to Tyson Foods as EVP, Chief Growth Officer, a newly created role, effective February 6. She will oversee global brand marketing, innovation, R&D, and communications for Tyson's portfolio. Boulden previously led Coca-Cola's Venturing & Emerging Brands unit and served as President, Still Beverages Business Unit, and her shift comes as Tyson Foods reported record sales despite flat or declining volumes in some segments.

title: Best Company Culture 2022
summary: Comparably announced its 6th Annual Best Places to Work series, recognizing companies with the best cultures as rated by their own employees. The rankings are based on millions of anonymous employee ratings across nearly 20 workplace metrics, covering compensation, work-life balance, leadership, and career growth. The report differentiates between Top 100 Large companies (over 500 employees) and Top 100 Small/Mid-size companies (500 or fewer employees), with detailed methodology provided for the selection process.

title: You Need A Design System — Here’s Why
summary: The article highlights the growing importance of design systems in organizations, driven by the increasing recognition of design's business value. It explains that design systems democratize design practice, support accessibility efforts, and improve talent retention by enabling designers to focus on more challenging and innovative work rather than repetitive tasks. The author encourages companies to adopt and effectively utilize design systems.

title: Scale up with hyper-personalization
summary: Hyper-personalization uses AI and machine learning to deliver tailored customer experiences at scale, leading to increased customer loyalty, revenue growth, and marketing ROI. The article outlines four key steps to implement hyper-personalization: collecting detailed customer data, organizing and synthesizing this data, creating targeted campaigns, and continuously analyzing progress. It also provides examples of how companies like Panera Bread, Signet Jewelers, U.S. Bank, EY, and NRG Energy have successfully leveraged hyper-personalization with Adobe solutions to enhance customer engagement and business outcomes.

title: Yamamoto brings 3D in-house with Adobe Substance 3D Stager
summary: Yamamoto, a full-service creative agency, has successfully integrated Adobe Substance 3D Stager into its workflow to bring 3D artwork production in-house. This strategic move has enabled the agency to enhance its services, offer more value to clients like Cargill and Analog Devices, and explore new business opportunities. By using Substance 3D Stager, Yamamoto has streamlined its creative process, reduced reliance on external vendors, and achieved significant cost and time savings while maintaining high-quality output.

title: Earnings Previews: Accenture, Adobe, Darden Restaurants
summary: This article previews the upcoming earnings reports for Accenture, Adobe, and Darden Restaurants.
